tasks.description:

tasks.title: Stamp records with source-chain provenance — schema hashes + lineage + era-scoped authority
tasks.description:
Per DEC-106: records need source-chain provenance, not just schema hashes. Each record must carry:
1. Schema + mapping hashes (#1541 — which ICD version produced this record)
2. Source provider chain — the full lineage (e.g., camera → Windows PC → Picasa → Google Photos → Takeout export)
3. Era tag — which source is authoritative for this record's time period (Takeout for pre-2008, iPhone for 2008+)

Platform migrations (Windows→Mac, Picasa→Google, iCloud sync) are data quality events — metadata can degrade at each boundary. The provenance chain prevents chasing enrichments that the source never captured.

## AC
- [ ] story-harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] notes-harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] photos harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] Each record carries icd:sourceChain — ordered list of systems it passed through
- [ ] Each record carries icd:authoritativeSource — era-scoped (which provider is golden for this time period)
- [ ] SPARQL query can find records produced under a specific hash pair
- [ ] SPARQL query can find records by authoritative source era
- [ ] icd-consistency-test.py --hashes verifies instance provenance matches current hashes

## Files
- src/services/story-harvester.service.ts
- src/services/notes-harvester.service.ts
- scripts/build-canonical-photos.py
- src/services/icd.service.ts (getVersionHashes call)
- scripts/icd-consistency-test.py
- src/ontology/icd-instance-photos.ttl (provider chain model)

tasks.title: Development framework — gate suite for agent software development lifecycle
tasks.description:
## Experience
Roles follow a structured development lifecycle — like the integration framework (ICD, mapper, validation) but for code changes. No guessing, no solo debugging, no declaring done without proof.

## AC
1. Memory-and-research gate (#1811) — check decisions, memory, git history before any code change
2. Root cause gate (#1815) — cross-role consultation with data (logs, errors, code refs) before coding a fix
3. Prove-it gate (#1812) — "cant be done" requires evidence, not assertion
4. Definition-of-done gate (#1814) — verified working against the symptom, not just code written and built
5. TDD enforced — unit, integration, and page-level tests on all code cards (DEC-1674)
6. Service design updated — every shipped card updates its service design page if touched
7. ICD updated — data flow changes update ICD
8. Blast radius and domain radius checks pass
9. Structured logging — product, value stream, domain, card, code object, datetime, version on every log line
10. Use lc/lw/ls/lk/nudge/chat when stuck — tools before guesses
11. All gates implemented as PreToolUse hooks that block, not reminders that suggest
12. Framework documented as a single reference page roles can read at session start

tasks.title: Root cause gate hook — require cross-role consultation with data before coding a fix
tasks.description:
## AC
1. PreToolUse hook detects when a role begins editing code to fix a defect
2. Hook checks session history for a /chat or brief exchange with at least one other role about the problem
3. The exchange must include data — logs, error output, code references — not just description of symptoms
4. If no cross-role consultation with data found, hook blocks with message: discuss root cause with another role first, bring data not guesses
5. Tests cover: block solo fix attempt, block consultation without data, pass with data-backed cross-role discussion

tasks.title: Verification gate hook — block Done declaration without proof of fix
tasks.description:
## AC
1. PreToolUse hook detects when a role moves a card to Done or declares a fix complete
2. Hook checks session history for verification step — actual test of the fix, not just build success
3. If no verification found, hook blocks with message: verify the fix works before declaring done
4. Build passing is not verification — the symptom must be retested
5. Tests cover: block without verification, pass with verification present

tasks.title: Clearing session tailer whitelist — invert filter logic to show only Jeff-facing content
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Session tailer uses a whitelist (what Jeff should see) not a blacklist (what to hide)
2. Role-to-role nudges, /chat messages, DELIVERED confirmations, and nudge.sh refs never appear in Clearing
3. Only Jeff-authored messages and role responses addressed to Jeff flow through
4. Tests cover: role-to-role chat filtered, nudge filtered, Jeff message passes, role response to Jeff passes
5. No new filter needed when a new role-to-role message type is added

tasks.title: Prove-it gate hook — block 'cant be done' assertions without evidence
tasks.description:
## AC
1. PreToolUse hook detects when a role asserts something is impossible or cant be done
2. Hook blocks with message: prove it — show the constraint, the error, or the code path
3. Assertion without evidence is not accepted — role must demonstrate the limitation
4. Hook passes when evidence (error output, code reference, platform constraint) is provided
5. Tests cover: block bare assertion, pass with evidence attached

tasks.title: Memory-and-research gate hook — PreToolUse block on writes without prior memory/git check
tasks.description:
## AC
1. PreToolUse hook fires before Edit/Write to code files
2. Hook checks session history for memory search (MEMORY.md, decisions, briefs) AND research (git log/show, Read of current state)
3. If neither found, hook blocks with message: check memory and research first
4. Hook passes silently when both checks detected
5. Tests cover: block when no prior check, pass when memory checked, pass when both checked

tasks.title: Chorus hooks code review — 14 findings from Kade audit
tasks.description:
## Code Review Findings (Kade, 2026-03-26)

### Bugs (fix)
1. **write_scrubber.rs:39 — CONTAINER_ID_RE false positives.** `[a-f0-9]{12,64}` matches git SHAs, UUIDs in shared files. Constant warn noise. Tighten pattern or exclude known-safe contexts.
2. **csc_guard.rs:12 — ARTIFACT_PATTERN too broad.** "extract" and "convert" are common words. `npm run convert` false-positives. Require proximity to file path or /tmp.
3. **main.rs:136-141 — batch_progress result silently dropped.** Checked but never returned. Surface the stderr or remove dead check.

### Edge Cases (harden)
4. **accept_gate.rs:34 — Role::Unknown allows everything.** If DEPLOY_ROLE unset, gate is wide open. Block Unknown from /acp.
5. **autonomy_guard.rs:402 — reads entire JSONL into memory.** Large sessions waste memory. Read from end.
6. **autonomy_guard.rs:267 — question-mark bypass.** Jeff's "fix tests?" skips permission-seeking detection. May be intentional but document.

### Dead Code (clean)
7. **types.rs:156 — permission_ask_json never called.** Remove.
8. **shim.rs — duplicate dispatch tables.** Lines 40-96 and 108-179 are identical. Consolidate.

### Code Quality (improve)
9. **write_scrubber.rs:45 — add parens for operator precedence clarity.** Works correctly but reads ambiguously.
10. **search_hierarchy.rs:85 — SQLite errors silently swallowed.** Log when Chorus DB is locked/corrupt.
11. **observer.rs:20 — /tmp/claude-team-scan assumed to exist.** Add create_dir_all.
12. **types.rs:119-124 — deny() uses exit_code 0.** Inconsistent with block_with_stderr (exit_code 2). Two blocking mechanisms confuse.
13. **search_hierarchy.rs is_code_lookup — too permissive.** snake_case classifier lets most searches bypass Chorus. Reduces DEC-074 effectiveness.
14. **infra_guardrails.rs — new, needs edge case review.**

## AC
- [ ] CONTAINER_ID_RE pattern tightened or context-aware (no false positives on git SHAs)
- [ ] ARTIFACT_PATTERN requires proximity to output path, not just keyword presence
- [ ] batch_progress result surfaced or dead check removed
- [ ] Role::Unknown blocked from /acp
- [ ] permission_ask_json removed
- [ ] shim.rs dispatch consolidated
- [ ] observer.rs creates /tmp/claude-team-scan if missing

## What
Document and operationalize Borg's two-phase delivery pattern as a repeatable methodology.

## Phase 1: Iterate with client (spike/discovery)
- Hand drawings → HTML prototypes, rapidly iterated
- Refine UI + OWL + value stream + domains as we go
- 2-3 cycles
- Identify integrations, security, and harvest considerations along the way

## Phase 2: Build from 1 (commitment)
- The iteration output becomes the spec — estimable, buildable
- Code + tests + UX + tickets + logs + monitoring + security + documentation
- Full blast radius coverage (DEC-072)

## Why
This is Jeff's natural working pattern — validated by the Athena experience at Staples. Phase 1 is discovery through making. Phase 2 is commitment informed by understanding. Maps to spike/discovery/commitment taxonomy.

## Athena lesson
Process weight kills adoption. Phase 1 must stay lightweight — sketches, conversation, rapid cycles. Phase 2 inherits rigor from the system, not from human paperwork.

## AC

tasks.title: Nudge wake — background poller that wakes idle roles when nudged, not just drain-on-prompt
tasks.description:
Nudge delivery currently requires the target role to have an active session AND submit a prompt to drain the inbox. If the role is idle, nudges pile up unread.

## AC
1. A background process polls each role's nudge inbox on a short interval (≤10s)
2. When a nudge arrives for an idle role, the poller wakes that role's terminal session automatically
3. Nudge delivery works whether the target role is active, idle, or has no session — no silent drops
4. Zero config for the sender — `/nudge <role> <message>` just works, like `ls`
5. No focus steal, no clipboard injection — wake mechanism respects Jeff's foreground context
6. Works across all three roles without per-role setup

## Files
- messages/scripts/nudge.sh
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/ (Rust shim nudge mode)
- LaunchAgents (new poller agent if needed)

tasks.title: Evaluate local LLM for Claude Code — Ollama on Bedroom for lightweight coding tasks

## Why it matters
- Ollama already runs on Bedroom for embeddings — same infra could host a coding model
- Privacy: Self domain data stays entirely local
- Cost: save API budget for Opus-level work, use local model for housekeeping (lint, simple edits, test runs)
- Could enable a "local-only" tier for Gathering's concentric trust model (DEC: Chorus=cloud, Gathering=hybrid, Self=local)

## AC
1. Evaluate: run Qwen3 Coder (or similar) on Bedroom via Ollama for simple Claude Code tasks
2. Test: can it run board-ts commands, lint fixes, simple file edits reliably?
3. Compare: quality/speed vs Anthropic API for housekeeping tasks
4. If viable: document setup, assess which tasks can route to local vs cloud
5. Privacy assessment: which domains could benefit from local-only processing

## Reference
https://www.xda-developers.com/wrote-script-run-claude-code-local-llm-skipping-cloud/
